content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807, SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662        322-5660           322-5901          322-4441

June 12, 1985


Gertrude Lotz
Councilmember, City of Suisun City
410 Avalon Way
Suisun City, CA  94585

                                        Re: Your Request for Advice
                                            Our File No. A-85-112

Dear Ms. Lotz:

     Thank you for your letter requesting advice as to whether
Suisun City may adopt an ordinance which would only require gifts
valued at $250 or more to be disclosed on statements of
economic interests.  The Political Reform Act requires gifts
valued at $50 or more to be disclosed on statements of economic
interests.

     Local agencies may impose additional disclosure require-
ments to those in the Political Reform Act so long as the
additional requirements do not prevent compliance with the Act.
(Government Code Section 81013.)  However, a local government
may not enact an ordinance which differs from and supersedes the
Political Reform Act.  See Edwin L. Miller Opinion, 2 FPPC
Opinions 91 (No. 75-125, July 6, 1976 (enclosed).

     Suisun City does not have the authority to enact an
ordinance which will raise the gift disclosure threshold of the
Political Reform Act.  A copy of this letter and its enclosures
are being sent to your city attorney.

     We appreciate your bringing this to our attention.  Should
you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to
contact this office.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Jeanette E. Turvill
                                        Legal Assistant

JET:plh
Enclosure
cc:  John Coyle, City Attorney

Suisun City, Ca. 94585
410 Avalon Way,
May 13, 1985

Fair Political Practices Comm,
1100 K St.,
Sacramento, Ca. 95817

Gentlemen;

I am a City Councilwoman for the City of Suisun City. I have a question regarding gifts.

Our Mayor is attempting to pass an Ordinance stating gifts of $250.00 or less are not reportable in our disclosure statements. I question the legality of this statement. I called the California League of Cities and they referred me to your group.

I will appreciate hearing from you with regards to this practice.

Sincerely,

Gertrude Lotz

sections.question: Whether Suisun City may adopt an ordinance which would only require gifts valued at $250 or more to be disclosed on statements of economic interests.
sections.conclusion: Suisun City does not have the authority to enact an ordinance which will raise the gift disclosure threshold of the Political Reform Act. Local agencies may impose additional disclosure requirements beyond the Political Reform Act so long as the additional requirements do not prevent compliance with the Act, but a local government may not enact an ordinance which differs from and supersedes the Political Reform Act.
sections.question_synthetic: Can a local government agency enact an ordinance that raises the gift disclosure threshold from $50 to $250, making gifts under $250 non-reportable?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Local agencies cannot enact ordinances that raise the gift disclosure threshold above the $50 required by the Political Reform Act, as this would supersede state law. While local agencies may add stricter disclosure requirements, they cannot relax or modify the state's minimum threshold.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (dated May 13, 1985) and FPPC's response letter (dated June 12, 1985). Clear question and conclusion sections present. File number A-85-112 matches document ID 85112.

QUESTION: Whether Suisun City may adopt an ordinance which would only require gifts valued at $250 or more to be disclosed on statements of economic interests.

CONCLUSION: Suisun City does not have the authority to enact an ordinance which will raise the gift disclosure threshold of the Political Reform Act. Local agencies may impose additional disclosure requirements beyond the Political Reform Act so long as the additional requirements do not prevent compliance with the Act, but a local government may not enact an ordinance which differs from and supersedes the Political Reform Act.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises Gertrude Lotz, a Suisun City Councilmember, that the city lacks authority to adopt an ordinance raising the gift disclosure threshold from $50 to $250 under the Political Reform Act.
